task_completion.description: Navigate to the WooCommerce Orders list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress admin dashboard is visible with 'WooCommerce' -> 'Orders' selected in the left sidebar, displaying a list of recent orders.

task_completion.description: Open the order details for Maria Garcia
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Order' or 'Order details' page is open, and 'Maria Garcia' is visible in the Billing or Shipping details section.

task_completion.description: Change the order status to Completed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Status' dropdown menu in the order details section is set to 'Completed'.

task_completion.description: Enter the exact tracking note content
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add note' text area contains the exact text: 'Shipped via Express. Tracking: TRACK-2024-78542'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the note is set as a private note
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menu below the 'Add note' text area is set to 'Private note' (not 'Note to customer').

task_completion.description: Add the note and save the order updates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note appears in the 'Order notes' sidebar list (typically with a grey background indicating it is private) and there is an 'Order updated.' success message at the top of the screen.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser to interact with the WooCommerce interface
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser window is visible displaying the WordPress/WooCommerce admin interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or WP-CLI to update the order
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows, command prompts, or SSH sessions are visible executing 'wp wc' or MySQL commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Elements, Console, etc.) are not open and being used to alter the DOM text.
